derivation_basis.design_bounds: calibration parameter grid limits tilts to +/-2pp weights and +/-1pp risk_tilt around the 50/50 baseline, bounding one-way turnover per rebalance at ~0.05; monthly cadence bounds the design-maximum annualized one-way turnover at ~0.60
derivation_basis.empirical_status: NOT empirically validated - no harness run exists yet; every threshold below is a design-and-domain-reasoned candidate that MUST be confronted with measured harness output before quant sign-off
derivation_basis.market_anchors: balanced multi-asset reference drawdowns (COVID_2020 ~20%, INFLATION_SHOCK_2022 ~20-25%, GFC_2008 ~30-35%) and typical tactical-allocation-overlay volatility of 6-12% annualized
derivation_basis.profile_semantics: conservative = strategy must behave strictly better than a balanced benchmark; base = strategy may match balanced-benchmark risk; aggressive = outer tolerance beyond which behavior is outside design intent regardless of preference

profiles.derivation: turnover at half the design ceiling assuming 2-4 quadrant transitions/year; drawdown/volatility set below balanced-benchmark stress outcomes so the overlay must demonstrably dampen risk

turnover equals the design ceiling (12 rebalances x 0.05 one-way) - exceeding it means the model is behaving outside its parameter-grid design; drawdown/volatility match balanced-benchmark historical stress outcomes
outer tolerance anchored to GFC-class balanced-benchmark drawdown; any breach here is unconditionally disqualifying because it exceeds worst-case design intent

recommendation_note: base profile is recommended for the first controlled activation: conservative risks rejecting a sound strategy on benchmark-level stress alone, aggressive tolerates behavior the parameter grid was never designed to produce. Final choice is the quant_owner's decision at review time, informed by harness measurements.
